title: calendula suffruticosa subsp. fulgida
intro: Calendula suffruticosa subsp. fulgida, also known as the Orange Marigold, is a subspecies of the Calendula suffruticosa plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family. The plant is known for its bright orange flowers and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine. The medicinal properties of Calendula suffruticosa subsp. fulgida include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce pain and inflammation, and prevent infection. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as salves, creams, and teas, and is often used to treat skin conditions, minor wounds, and digestive issues, offering a range of health benefits.
